1920, in the electronics sense, "the return of a fraction of an output signal to the input of an earlier stage," from verbal phrase, from feed (v.) + back (adv.).

Old English timber "building, structure," in late Old English "building material, trees suitable for building," and "trees or woods in general," from Proto-Germanic *timran (source also of Old Saxon timbar "a building, room," Old Frisian timber "wood, building," Old High German zimbar "timber, wooden dwelling, room," Old Norse timbr "timber," German Zimmer "room"), from PIE *deme- "to build," possibly from root *dem- "house, household" (source of Greek domos, Latin domus; see domestic (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 相似/相同 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1620s, "feign, pretend, assume falsely" (implied in simulated), back-formation from simulation or else from Latin simulatus, past participle of simulare "to make like, imitate, copy." Meaning "to use a model to imitate certain conditions for purposes of study or training" is from 1947.

morphology.mnemonic: in + 关闭 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: "characterized by including a great deal, leaving little out," c.

mid-14c., "specially prepared or arranged display," from Old French spectacle "sight, spectacle, Roman games" (13c.), from Latin spectaculum "a public show, spectacle, place from which shows are seen," from spectare "to view, watch, behold," frequentative form of specere "to look at," from PIE *spek- "to observe" (see scope (n.1))..
1590s, "power to accomplish something," from Latin efficientia "efficient power; efficiency; influence" (from efficientem; see efficient) + -cy.

meanings.definitionCn: 在头顶上的； 架空的 A

etymology.story: 1530s, "above one's head" (adv.), from over- + head.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 抓住/理解 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French comprehénsion (15c.), from Latin comprehensionem (nominative comprehensio) "a seizing, laying hold of, arrest," figuratively "perception, comprehension," noun of action from past participle stem of comprehendere (see comprehend).

meanings.definitionCn: 完整无缺的,未经触动的,未受损伤的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin intactus "untouched, uninjured; undefiled, chaste; unsubdued," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + tactus, past participle of tangere "to touch" (see tangent (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 下列的，下述的，其次的，接着的

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of following, an act of following," verbal noun from follow (v.).

1620s, "young, youthful," from Latin iuvenilis "of or belonging to youth, youthful," from iuvenis "young person, one in the flower of his or her age" (in Roman use, the period just beyond adolescence, from age 21 or 25 to 40), noun use of an adjective meaning "young" (source also of French jeune; see young (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有同情心的；赞同的；n.交感神经

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/一起 + 感情/疾病 + ic

etymology.story: 1640s, "pertaining to sympathy," from Modern Latin sympatheticus, from late Greek sympathetikos "having sympathy," from sympathein, from sympathes "having a fellow feeling, affected by like feelings" (see sympathy).
"to load (a ship) with goods or merchandise for shipment," mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: （在内）变动；平行，列为一行；延伸；漫游；射程达到

etymology.story: 1706, from sports + man (n.).

late 14c., from Old French deriver "to flow, pour out; derive, originate," from Latin derivare "to lead or draw off (a stream of water) from its source" (in Late Latin also "to derive"), from phrase de rivo (de "from" + rivus "stream;" see rivulet).

使生裂缝，使有裂纹；使无效；使有缺陷

维护，坚持；断言；主张；声称
拍打，拍打声；神经紧张；[航] 襟翼
拍动；神经紧张；鼓翼而飞；（帽边等）垂下

meanings.definitionCn: (to)反对,异议,不喜欢,反对的理由

morphology.mnemonic: 对/向 + 投/掷 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French objeccion "reply, retort" (12c.) and directly from Late Latin obiectionem (nominative obiectio), "a throwing or putting before," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin obicere "to oppose" (see object (n.))..
1828 (from 1822 as a French word in English), from French blouse, "workman's or peasant's smock" (1788), origin unknown.

root.meaning: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow

morphology.mnemonic: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "performance of a religious rite," from Latin observationem (nominative observatio) "a watching over, observance, investigation," noun of action from past participle stem of observare (see observe).

morphology.mnemonic: in + dwell居住 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "one who dwells in a place" (as distinguished from a visitor or transient), early 15c., from Anglo-French inhabitant, from Latin inhabitantem (nominative inhabitans), present participle of inhabitare "to dwell in" (see inhabit).

meanings.definitionCn: 碎片,小部分,一点儿;分数

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., originally in the mathematical sense, from Anglo-French fraccioun (Old French fraccion, "a breaking," 12c., Modern French fraction) and directly from Late Latin fractionem (nominative fractio) "a breaking," especially into pieces, in Medieval Latin "a fragment, portion," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin frangere "to break (something) in pieces, shatter, fracture," from Proto-Italic *frang-, from a nasalized variant of PIE root *bhreg- "to break" (source also of Sanskrit (giri)-bhraj "breaking-forth (out of the mountains);" Gothic brikan, Old English brecan "to break;" Lithuanian brasketi "crash, crack;" Old Irish braigim "break wind").

meanings.definitionCn: 象征的；表意的；作为对某事的保证的
滤波器；[化工] 过滤器；筛选；滤光器

etymology.story: 1570s (transitive), from French filtrer or from Medieval Latin filtrare, from filtrum "felt" (see filter (n.)).

1570s, "a (social) moving, stirring, agitation," from Middle French émotion (16c.), from Old French emouvoir "stir up" (12c.), from Latin emovere "move out, remove, agitate," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + movere "to move" (see move (v.)).

early 15c., "to fix the amount (of a tax, fine, etc.)," from Anglo-French assesser, from Medieval Latin assessare "fix a tax upon," originally frequentative of Latin assessus "a sitting by," past participle of assidere "to sit beside" (and thus to assist in the office of a judge), from ad- "to" (see ad-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 写 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., in law, "the right to something through long use," from Old French prescription (13c.) and directly from Latin praescriptionem (nominative praescriptio) "a writing before, order, direction," noun of action from past participle stem of praescribere "write before, prefix in writing; ordain, determine in advance," from prae "before" (see pre-) + scribere "to write" (see script (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 看/观察 + 使…/具有…的 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1580s, from Latin spectator "viewer, watcher," from past participle stem of spectare "to view, watch" (see spectacle).

meanings.definitionCn: 行为， 举止；特性； 运转情况 A
论证；论据；争吵；内容提要

morphology.mnemonic: to oppose, dispute + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: early 14c., "statements and reasoning in support of a proposition," from Old French arguement "reasoning, opinion; accusation, charge" (13c.), from Latin argumentum "evidence, ground, support, proof; a logical argument," from arguere "to argue" (see argue).

meanings.definitionCn: 完全的；彻底的；无条件的
充足的，足够的；适当的，胜任的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin adaequatus "equalized," past participle of adaequare "to make equal to," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + aequare "make level," from aequus (see equal (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 香肠；腊肠；装香肠的碎肉

etymology.story: mid-15c., sawsyge, from Old North French saussiche (Modern French saucisse), from Vulgar Latin *salsica "sausage," from salsicus "seasoned with salt," from Latin salsus "salted" (see sauce)..

meanings.definitionCn: 极大的，巨大的；惊人的；极好的

etymology.story: 1630s, "awful, dreadful, terrible," from Latin tremendus "fearful, to be dreaded, terrible," literally "to be trembled at," gerundive form of tremere "to tremble" (see tremble (v.)).

early 15c., from Middle French condenser (14c.) or directly from Latin condensare "to make dense," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + densare "make thick," from densus "dense, thick, crowded," a word used of crowds, darkness, clouds, etc.

1670s, from French hygiène, ultimately from Greek hygieine techne "the healthful art," from hygies "healthy, sound, hearty," literally "living well" (personified as the goddess Hygieia), from PIE *eyu-gwie-es- "having a vigorous life," from root *aiw-, *ayu- "vital force, life, long life, eternity; in the prime of life, young" (source of Latin aevus, English ever; see eon).

meanings.definitionCn: 司法，法律制裁；正义；法官，审判员

etymology.story: mid-12c., "the exercise of authority in vindication of right by assigning reward or punishment;" also "quality of being fair and just; moral soundness and conformity to truth," from Old French justice "justice, legal rights, jurisdiction" (11c.), from Latin iustitia "righteousness, equity," from iustus "upright, just" (see just (adj.)).
1540s, from Latin conspicuus "visible, open to view, striking," from conspicere "to look at, observe, see, notice," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + specere (see scope (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 标签；名称；结束语；附属物
尾随，紧随；连接；起浑名；添饰

etymology.story: late Old English (replacing Old English fers, an early West Germanic borrowing directly from Latin), "line or section of a psalm or canticle," later "line of poetry" (late 14c.), from Anglo-French and Old French vers "line of verse; rhyme, song," from Latin versus "a line, row, line of verse, line of writing," from PIE root *wer- (3) "to turn, bend" (see versus).

late 14c., "impetuous, unrestrained," from Old French furios, furieus "furious, enraged, livid" (14c., Modern French furieux), from Latin furiosus "full of rage, mad," from furia "rage, passion, fury" (see fury).

1610s, from French obésité and directly from Latin obesitas "fatness, corpulence," from obesus "that has eaten itself fat," past participle of obdere "to eat all over, devour," from ob "over" (see ob-) + edere "eat" (see edible)..

meanings.definitionCn: （日晒后皮肤的）黝黑色；棕褐色；鞣料；马戏团

etymology.story: "bronze color imparted to skin by exposure to sun," 1749, see tan (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + watershed, separation between river valleys + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "portable folding case for holding papers," 1926, from brief (n.) in the paper sense + case (n.2)..
1771, from snow (n.) + storm (n.)..
early 14c., "obligatory contribution levied by a sovereign or government," from Anglo-French tax, Old French taxe, and directly from Medieval Latin taxa, from Latin taxare (see tax (v.)).

early 14c., constreyen, from stem of Old French constreindre (Modern French contraindre) "restrain, control," from Latin constringere "to bind together, tie tightly, fetter, shackle, chain," from com- "together" (see com-) + stringere "to draw tight" (see strain (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 兴奋；刺激；令人兴奋的事物

etymology.story: early 15c., "encouragement;" c.
also blue-print, 1882, from blue (1) + print (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 临时的，暂时的；中间的；间歇的
过渡时期，中间时期；暂定

etymology.story: "time intervening," 1560s, from Latin interim (adv.) "in the meantime, meanwhile," originally "in the midst of that," from inter- "between" (see inter-) + im, ancient adverb from stem of pronoun is "this, that." As an adjective from c.

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + bring about by unusual effort

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to drive by physical force; to try, attempt, strive; to fortify, strengthen a place;" late 14c.

morphology.mnemonic: separate division of a government + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Old French deporteure "departure," figuratively, "death," from departir (see depart) + -ure (see -ure)..

meanings.definitionCn: 易碎的，脆的，易损坏的；虚弱的，脆弱的

etymology.story: 1510s, "liable to sin, morally weak;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 映像， 倒影；反省， 沉思
驾驶室；出租汽车；出租马车

etymology.story: 1826,  "light, horse-drawn carriage," shortening of cabriolet (1763), from French  cabriolet (18c.), diminutive of cabrioler "leap, caper" (16c./17c.), from Italian capriolare "jump in the air," from capriola, properly "the leap of a kid," from Latin capreolus "wild goat, roebuck," from PIE *kap-ro- "he-goat, buck" (source also of Old Irish gabor, Welsh gafr, Old English hæfr, Old Norse hafr "he-goat").

the science of social phenomena, 1842, from French sociologie, a hybrid coined 1830 by French philosopher Isidore Auguste Comte (1798-1857), from Latin socius "associate" (see social (adj.)) + Greek-derived suffix -logie (see -logy)..
1610s, "pertaining to giants," from Latin gigant- stem of gigas "giant" (see giant) + -ic.

Old English fleon, flion "take flight, fly from, avoid, escape" (contracted class II strong verb; past tense fleah, past participle flogen), from Proto-Germanic *fleuhan "to run away" (source also of Old High German fliohan, Old Norse flöja, Old Frisian flia, Dutch vlieden, German fliehen, Gothic þliuhan "to flee"), probably from PIE *pleuk-, extended form of root *pleu- "to flow" (see pluvial).

meanings.definitionCn: 明显的；独特的；清楚的；有区别的

etymology.story: late 14c., originally past participle of distincten (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 用树篱笆围住；避免作正面答复
对冲，套期保值；树篱；障碍
哭泣；流泪；哀悼；滴落；渗出液体

etymology.story: Old English wepan "shed tears, cry; bewail, mourn over; complain" (class VII strong verb; past tense weop, past participle wopen), from Proto-Germanic *wopjan (source also of Old Norse op, Old High German wuof "shout, shouting, crying," Old Saxon wopian, Gothic wopjan "to shout, cry out, weep"), from PIE *wab- "to cry, scream" (source also of Latin vapulare "to be flogged;" Old Church Slavonic vupiti "to call," vypu "gull").
1540s, "to raise, lift, elevate," especially with a rope or tackle, earlier hoise (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 意识到的；知道的；有…方面知识的；懂世故的

etymology.story: late Old English gewær, from Proto-Germanic *ga-waraz (source also of Old Saxon giwar, Middle Dutch gheware, Old High German giwar, German gewahr), from *ga-, intensive prefix, + waraz "wary, cautious" (see wary)..

meanings.definitionCn: 数值的；数字的；用数字表示的（等于numeric）

morphology.mnemonic: 数/数量 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin numerus "a number" (see number (n.)) + suffix -ical.

meanings.definitionCn: 戏剧； 剧本；戏剧性事件或场面

etymology.story: 1510s, from Late Latin drama "play, drama," from Greek drama (genitive dramatos) "play, action, deed," from dran "to do, act, perform" (especially some great deed, whether good or bad), from PIE *dere- "to work." Drama queen attested by 1992..
late 14c., "decision after consideration," from Anglo-French award, Old French esguard, from esguarder (see award (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 符合条件的;(尤指婚姻等)合适(意)的

etymology.story: early 15c., "fit or proper to be chosen," from Old French eligible "fit to be chosen" (14c.), from Late Latin eligibilis "that may be chosen," from Latin eligere "choose" (see election).

mid-15c., "heated room, bath-room," from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch stove, both meaning "heated room," which was the original sense in English; a general West Germanic word (Old English stofa "bath-room," Old High German stuba, German Stube "sitting room").

meanings.definitionCn: 对...作出的评价；评价，鉴定，评估

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "setting of a price," by 1784, American English, from appraise + -al (2).
"to clear the ground of weeds," late Old English weodian "to weed," from the source of weed (n.).

1744, from French glacier (16c.), from Savoy dialect glacière "moving mass of ice," from Old French glace "ice," from Vulgar Latin *glacia (source also of Old Provençal glassa, Italian ghiaccia), from Latin glacies "ice" (see glacial).

meanings.definitionCn: 方向；定向；适应；情况介绍；向东方

morphology.mnemonic: determine bearings + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1839, originally "arrangement of a building, etc., to face east or any other specified direction," noun of action from orient (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 空闲的， 闲置的；无用的， 无效的；懒散的

etymology.story: 1550s, past participle adjective from limit (v.); as a stand-alone for limited express train, by 1883.

"to pledge" (obsolete except in archaic plight one's troth), from Old English pligtan, plihtan "to endanger, imperil, compromise," verb form of pliht (n.) "danger, risk," from PIE root *dlegh- "to engage oneself," forming words in Celtic, Germanic, Slavic, and possibly Latin (see play (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 致敬；招呼；向欢呼；猛发；使像下雹样落下（过去式hailed，过去分词hailed，现在分词hailing，第三人称单数hails）

etymology.story: "frozen rain, pellets of ice falling in showers," Old English hægl, hagol (Mercian hegel) "hail, hailstorm," also the name of the rune for H, from Proto-Germanic *haglaz (source also of Old Frisian heil, Old Saxon, Old High German hagal, Old Norse hagl, German Hagel "hail"), probably from PIE *kaghlo- "pebble" (source also of Greek kakhlex "round pebble")..
mid-14c., "causing fear," from fear (n.) + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 口音；重音；强调；特点；重音符号

etymology.story: "to pronounce with accent or stress," 1520s, from Middle French accenter, from Old French acenter, from acent (see accent (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 被动的，消极的；被动语态的

etymology.story: late 14c., in grammatical sense (opposed to active), Old French passif "suffering, undergoing hardship" (14c.) and directly from Latin passivus "capable of feeling or suffering," from pass-, past participle stem of pati "to suffer" (see passion).

meanings.definitionCn: （英）卡车；[车辆] 货车；运料车

etymology.story: "a truck; a long, flat wagon," 1838, British railroad word, probably from verb lurry "to pull, tug" (1570s), of uncertain origin.
late 14c., agent noun from carry (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 种类，部属，类目；范畴，类型

etymology.story: 1580s, from Middle French catégorie, from Late Latin categoria, from Greek kategoria "accusation, prediction, category," verbal noun from kategorein "to speak against; to accuse, assert, predicate," from kata "down to" (or perhaps "against;" see cata-) + agoreuein "to harangue, to declaim (in the assembly)," from agora "public assembly" (see agora).
